title: Python & OOP Fundamentals
description: A beginner-friendly roadmap for learning Python from the ground up, with a focus on Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts. This path takes you from Python basics all the way through classes, inheritance, and design patterns.

phases.title: Phase 1: Python Basics
phases.description: Get comfortable with Python syntax, data types, and control flow before diving into OOP.

topics.name: Python Syntax & Data Types
topics.description: Learn Python's clean syntax and built-in data types: integers, floats, strings, booleans, lists, tuples, sets, and dictionaries.
keyConcepts: Type conversion and type checking with type()

projects.name: Data Organizer Script
projects.description: Write a Python script that stores student names and grades in a dictionary, calculates averages, and prints a formatted report.

topics.name: Control Flow & Functions
topics.description: Master conditionals, loops, and reusable functions — the building blocks of any Python program.
keyConcepts: for and while loops, break and continue
Defining functions with def, parameters, and return values

projects.name: Number Guessing Game
projects.description: Build a simple CLI number guessing game using loops and conditionals that tracks the number of attempts.
keyDeliverables: Use of while loop and break

phases.title: Phase 2: OOP Fundamentals
phases.description: Learn the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming — Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, and Polymorphism — using hands-on Python examples.

topics.name: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Understand how to define classes as blueprints and create objects (instances) from them. Learn about __init__, instance variables, and methods.
keyConcepts: Defining a class with the class keyword
Instance methods, class methods (@classmethod), and static methods (@staticmethod)

projects.name: Library Book System
projects.description: Create a Book class with title, author, and year attributes, plus methods to display info and check availability.

topics.name: Encapsulation
topics.description: Encapsulation bundles data (attributes) and methods that operate on that data inside a class, and restricts direct access from outside using access modifiers.

projects.name: BankAccount Class
projects.description: Build a BankAccount class with a private balance, and deposit/withdraw methods that validate inputs using the @property decorator.

topics.name: Inheritance
topics.description: Inheritance lets a child class reuse and extend the behavior of a parent class, avoiding code duplication.
keyConcepts: Multiple inheritance and MRO (Method Resolution Order)

projects.name: Animal Hierarchy
projects.description: Create an Animal base class, then subclasses Dog, Cat, and Bird that override a make_sound() method and add unique attributes.

topics.name: Polymorphism
topics.description: Polymorphism allows different classes to be treated through the same interface, with each class providing its own implementation.
keyConcepts: Duck typing — 'if it quacks like a duck...'
Abstract base classes (ABC) from the abc module

projects.name: Shape Area Calculator
projects.description: Define an abstract Shape base class with an abstract area() method, then implement Circle, Rectangle, and Triangle subclasses.
keyDeliverables: Loop through a list of mixed shapes and print areas

topics.name: Abstraction
topics.description: Abstraction hides complex internal details and exposes only what is necessary. In Python this is achieved through abstract classes and interfaces.

projects.name: Payment Gateway Interface
projects.description: Define an abstract PaymentProcessor class, then implement CreditCard and PayPal subclasses with their own process_payment() logic.

phases.title: Phase 3: Advanced OOP & Best Practices
phases.description: Level up with special methods, composition, and introductory design patterns.

topics.name: Magic / Dunder Methods
topics.description: Python's special methods (surrounded by double underscores) let you define how objects behave with built-in operations.

projects.name: Custom Vector Class
projects.description: Build a 2D Vector class that supports +, -, * operators, comparison, and a readable __repr__.

topics.name: Composition vs Inheritance
topics.description: Learn when to prefer composition ('has-a') over inheritance ('is-a') to write more flexible and maintainable code.

projects.name: RPG Character Builder
projects.description: Design an RPG character system using composition: a Character class that holds a Weapon, Armor, and Skill objects.
keyDeliverables: Method to display full character stats

topics.name: Intro to Design Patterns
topics.description: Get a beginner-level introduction to the most common OOP design patterns.
keyConcepts: Singleton pattern — ensure only one instance of a class
Factory pattern — delegate object creation to a factory method
Observer pattern — event-driven communication between objects
Strategy pattern — swap algorithms at runtime

resources.name: Refactoring Guru - Design Patterns in Python

projects.name: Logger Singleton
projects.description: Implement a Logger class using the Singleton pattern so the same logger instance is shared across your entire application.
